tldr: If your media is mostly video, api.video is a video-specialized platform with upload, encoding, streaming, player, webhooks, and analytics — narrower than Cloudinary but more focused on the video workflow.
why_switch: Your workload is video-first and Cloudinary's image features are unused
You want a video-native player, webhooks, and analytics out of the box
You prefer a vendor focused exclusively on video infrastructure
You want simpler pricing scoped to video use cases
You need streaming-oriented features more than image transformations
what_to_export: Original video assets (mp4 or source masters)
Webhook endpoints currently consuming Cloudinary events
export_method: Pull originals from Cloudinary using their official Admin API (resources endpoint) or the media library export — confirm current endpoints in Cloudinary docs. Download the highest-quality source files; do not rely on transformed derivatives as the master.
import_method: Upload to api.video via their official API or SDKs (e.g. @api.video/nodejs-client). Each upload returns a videoId and a player URL; map your old Cloudinary public_ids to api.video videoIds. Re-attach captions and metadata via the appropriate endpoints — consult api.video's official docs for the current schema.

concept_mapping.note: Maintain a mapping table so old links can redirect.
Transcoding is automatic; check docs for delivery format options.
Replace embed code; api.video provides its own player.
Confirm current upload patterns in official docs.
Different data model — verify the metrics you need are available.

effort: 1-3 weeks for a video-heavy library, longer if you have thousands of assets or complex player customizations.
gotchas: api.video is video-only — image assets need a different home
Player and CDN are tied to api.video — switching again later means re-embedding
Bandwidth and storage pricing differs — model on the official pricing page
Old Cloudinary URLs won't resolve after switch; plan redirects or maintain dual delivery during cutover
Captions/subtitles formats may need conversion — consult docs
Verify region/CDN coverage matches your audience

email.subject: Moving video assets from Cloudinary to api.video
email.body:
Hi,

Since our usage is mostly video, we're consolidating onto api.video. Plan: pull originals from Cloudinary, re-upload via api.video's API, swap player embeds, and re-register webhooks. We'll keep a Cloudinary->api.video ID map for redirects. Targeting a 1-3 week migration window.

Thanks
